{{Short description="Branch of mechanics concerned with forces and their effect on motion}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Dynamics (mechanics)
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Classical mechanics]], [[Newton's laws of motion]], [[Force]], [[Momentum]], [[Kinematics]]
}}

'''Dynamics''' is the branch of [[classical mechanics]] concerned with the study of [[force]]s and their effects on [[motion]]. It is distinguished from [[kinematics]], which describes motion without reference to its causes, and [[statics]], which concerns forces in equilibrium resulting in no motion.

== Newton's laws of motion ==
{{Main|Newton's laws of motion}}
The foundation of classical dynamics is [[Newton's three laws]]:

# A body remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by a net force ([[inertia]])
# <math>\mathbf{F} = \frac{d\mathbf{p}}{dt}</math> — force equals rate of change of momentum
# For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction

=== Conservation of momentum ===
From Newton's second and third laws, the total [[momentum]] of an isolated system is conserved:
:<math>\sum \mathbf{p}_i = \text{constant}</math>

== Types of dynamics ==

=== Particle dynamics ===
For a single particle: <math>m\mathbf{a} = \mathbf{F}_{\text{net}}</math>

=== Rigid body dynamics ===
Considers both translation and rotation:
* Translation: <math>\mathbf{F}_{\text{net}} = m\mathbf{a}_{\text{CM}}</math>
* Rotation: <math>\boldsymbol{\tau}_{\text{net}} = I\boldsymbol{\alpha}</math>

=== Continuum dynamics ===
* [[Fluid dynamics]] — Navier–Stokes equations
* [[Elasticity (physics)|Elasticity]]
* [[Plasma (physics)|Plasma dynamics]]

== Analytical formulations ==

=== Lagrangian dynamics ===
{{Main|Lagrangian mechanics}}
:<math>\frac{d}{dt}\left(\frac{\partial L}{\partial \dot{q}_i}\right) - \frac{\partial L}{\partial q_i} = 0</math>

=== Hamiltonian dynamics ===
{{Main|Hamiltonian mechanics}}
:<math>\dot{q}_i = \frac{\partial H}{\partial p_i}, \quad \dot{p}_i = -\frac{\partial H}{\partial q_i}</math>

== Applications ==
* [[Orbital mechanics]] — spacecraft trajectories
* [[Vehicle dynamics]] — automotive and aerospace engineering
* [[Robotics]] — control of manipulators and mobile robots
* [[Structural dynamics]] — building and bridge response to loads
* [[Vibration analysis]] — machine and structural vibrations

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Classical mechanics]]
* [[Force]]
* [[Kinematics]]
* [[Lagrangian mechanics]]
* [[Newton's laws of motion]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Dynamics (mechanics)| ]]
[[Category:Classical mechanics]]
